---
title: Build with resq-flow
description: The practical side of running resq-flow, wiring it into the stack, and extending it with new flows.
---

This section is the practical side of the docs.

Use it when you need to:

- run `resq-flow` locally
- use the CLI for relay checks and flow logs from a terminal
- connect it to the shared observability stack
- understand what `resq-agent` needs to emit
- add a new flow
- validate or troubleshoot the setup

## The practical sequence

For most teammates, the order looks like this:

1. start with [Local setup](/docs/build/local-setup)
2. read [Using the CLI](/docs/build/cli) if you want terminal-first or agent-friendly inspection
3. read [Using with resq-agent](/docs/build/resq-agent-integration)
4. use [Adding a flow](/docs/build/adding-a-flow) when you need to ship a new flow
5. fall back to [Troubleshooting](/docs/build/troubleshooting) when the stack is up but not behaving correctly
